hyperedges.label: Book Ingestion Pipeline (Gutendex to bundled JSON)

nodes.label: React Native + Expo SDK 54 Stack
nodes.rationale: Chosen over an earlier Kotlin Multiplatform + Compose Multiplatform plan because the user's dev environment is VS Code, not Android Studio/IntelliJ; RN+Expo has first-class VS Code support and Expo Go lets you iterate on a physical phone with near-zero native setup.

nodes.label: Kotlin Multiplatform + Compose Multiplatform (rejected plan)
nodes.rationale: Earlier plan, rejected because CMP's tooling assumes a JetBrains IDE, incompatible with the user's VS Code environment.

nodes.label: SDK Version Pinning (SDK 54)
nodes.rationale: Pinned to Expo SDK 54 because that's what the phone's Expo Go build supported when this was set up; a mismatched SDK causes 'Incompatible SDK version' at launch, fixed by updating Expo Go or re-pinning via expo install --fix.

nodes.label: Expo Router Hot-Reload Gotcha
nodes.rationale: Deleting/adding/restructuring route files does not reliably hot-reload; fix is to stop Metro and restart with npx expo start -c, then reopen Expo Go on the phone.

nodes.label: Design System (tokens.ts + typography.ts)
nodes.rationale: Locked design system, transcribed directly from the Figma Design System doc; not arbitrary, do not tweak ad hoc.

nodes.label: Color Palette (Flame Amber, Parchment, Primary Dark)
nodes.norm_label: color palette (flame amber, parchment, primary dark)

nodes.label: FlameGlow.tsx Brand Mark
nodes.rationale: Only a glow layer behind the logo pulses opacity; the mark itself stays fully static/opaque. Earlier version scaled+rotated the flame and dipped its opacity to 0, which looked like distortion/glitching — don't reintroduce that.

nodes.label: stripLeadingToc() Heuristic
nodes.rationale: Detects a run of >=3 suspiciously tightly-packed chapter-heading matches at the start of the text and drops the whole run, since duplicate TOC entries cluster far more tightly than any real chapter body, regardless of book length — this generalizes across books.

nodes.label: chapter1Anchor Override (Pride and Prejudice)
nodes.rationale: Only pride-and-prejudice needs an explicit chapter1Anchor because that Gutenberg edition's real Chapter 1 has no heading and sits after a critic's introduction essay. A generic 'paragraph length' heuristic was tried and reverted because it can't distinguish a real unheaded chapter 1 from a long editorial introduction.

nodes.label: paginate.ts Pagination Engine
nodes.rationale: Chapters are pre-chunked at ingestion time by character budget rather than measured against actual on-device rendered glyph layout — a pragmatic simplification versus the more-correct, much-harder real per-device text measurement approach.

nodes.label: ReaderMode Enum (Day / Lamp default / Lamp alt)
nodes.norm_label: readermode enum (day / lamp default / lamp alt)

nodes.label: WarmthPicker Component
nodes.rationale: A discrete 5-preset picker rather than a continuous drag slider — a pragmatic simplification.

nodes.label: Highlight Feature (paragraph-level long-press)
nodes.rationale: Long-press a paragraph rather than arbitrary text-range selection — a deliberate scope cut. The highlights table's start_offset/end_offset columns are repurposed to hold the paragraph index, not a character offset, when read back in the Reader.

nodes.label: PageTurnFlash.tsx Transition
nodes.rationale: A brief amber corner flash, not a 3D page-flip simulation, matching the design system's 'calm, not playful' motion rule.

nodes.label: Unofficial Google Translate Endpoint
nodes.rationale: Unofficial, key-free translate.googleapis.com endpoint; works well but could break or rate-limit without warning — a paid API is the durable fallback if that happens.

nodes.label: Milestone 7+ (Billing, ML Kit, Dev Client transition)
nodes.norm_label: milestone 7+ (billing, ml kit, dev client transition)

nodes.label: Verification Habits (tsc, expo export)
nodes.rationale: tsc --noEmit and expo export --platform android are automated smoke tests, but neither replaces actually checking the phone when the change is visual/interactive — ask the user what they see rather than assuming.

nodes.label: Expo Starter Tutorial Screenshot (Welcome to Expo, web)
nodes.norm_label: expo starter tutorial screenshot (welcome to expo, web)

nodes.label: Role & operating rules
nodes.rationale: Smallest possible diff, never refactor unrelated code, never touch native config or add dependencies without asking, ask instead of guessing — keeps changes reviewable and preserves an exact design match.

nodes.label: Color system (amber as single accent)
nodes.rationale: Amber is the one recurring accent (CTAs, active state, progress, bookmark, lamp glow) so it never becomes decorative; one accent per screen keeps the palette intimate/candlelit rather than flat/corporate.

nodes.label: Typography system (Lora/Manrope split)
nodes.rationale: Strict separation: Lora serif for anything that is the book, Manrope sans for app chrome. Reading body fixed at Lora 17px/1.85 line-height as 'the single most important number in the system' — never shrunk or tightened.

nodes.label: Before implementing a UI change (workflow)
nodes.rationale: Check tokens/typography, then existing components, then the design zip annotation, then ask — in that order — to avoid inventing values or duplicating components.

nodes.label: Phasing philosophy
nodes.rationale: Ship the smallest thing that lets 10-15 friends actually read and lets you measure what they do before building any Premium feature; Premium-shaped schema fields exist so later features are additive, never a risky migration.

nodes.label: Free tier philosophy — why caps are generous
nodes.rationale: Caps should sit above what a normal engaged reader hits in 2-4 weeks so a real habit forms before any wall appears — citing Evernote's 2-device sync limit as the cautionary tale and Duolingo's unlimited-core-lessons model as the one to copy. translations_per_day is set more generously than the per-book caps because it's a daily rate limit most likely to interrupt an immersive session and break first-session trust.
nodes.norm_label: free tier philosophy — why caps are generous

nodes.label: The Premium message
nodes.rationale: Don't market Premium as 'unlimited translations' — market it as helping you remember what you read; vocabulary, quotes, SRS, and insights all serve that one pitch.
